name: Roasted Squab with Corn and Chili Sauce
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
4 ounces slab bacon cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 large ripe tomato, seeded and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F for 20 minutes.
2. Season the squabs inside and out with salt and pepper.
3. Place juniper berries into the cavities and brush the squabs with olive oil.
4. Place breast-side down in a roasting pan and roast in the hot oven for 20 minutes until rare.
5. Meanwhile, cook cubed bacon over low heat in a heavy-bottomed saucepan until fully browned. Remove the bacon bits, leaving the bacon fat in the saucepan.
6. Add diced onion to the bacon fat and slowly sauté until translucent.
7. Cut corn from the cob and set kernels aside. Cut or break cobs to fit the saucepan and add them to the cooking onions.
8. Add chili paste to the onion/corn cob mixture and cook for 2-3 minutes.
9. Add maple syrup, vinegar, and red wine, and reduce by half.
10. Add remaining ingredients except reserved corn kernels. Cook another 15 minutes.
11. Remove corn cobs and add kernels. Cook 2 more minutes.
12. Serve sauce over roasted squabs.
Yield: 4 servings
